channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The latest iPhone 17 leak hints at one iPhone 16 feature that might not change
item.description: Previous rumors had pointed to a slimmed down Dynamic Island on the iPhone 17, but this contradicts them.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he will use energy emergency declaration to approve more power for AI
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that he will use his energy emergency declaration to approve power stations for artificial intelligence (AI) centers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Seeking growth, UK competition regulator probes Apple and Google
item.description: Apple and Google now face regulatory probes in the ailing UK market, where the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has confirmed it intends to investigate both company’s mobile ecosystems under new digital market laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The sickening truth: Healthcare data breaches reach all-time high
item.description: Healthcare records for more than 184 million Americans were breached in 2024, but you can take steps to minimize that risk. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle’s growing impact in the Middle East: Driving AI and cloud innovation
item.description: Oracle has played a pivotal role in the technological transformation of the Middle East over the past three decades, and the company’s presence in the UAE and Saudi Arabia continues to grow stronger.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? This is probably why
item.description: The official POTUS and White House accounts are managed by the White House and “change when the occupant of the White House changes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stratoshark brings Wireshark-style analysis to cloud system calls
item.description: The open-source Wireshark network protocol analyzer has been a standard tool for networking professionals for decades. In recent years, organizations have increasingly moved workloads to the cloud, where they have not had the same network visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech titans sound off on Trump's AI project
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For the new Congress, an AI proposal from the last Congress
item.description: Members of the bipartisan House Task Force on AI want to ensure U.S. leadership while mitigating the threats to privacy and safety. The post For the new Congress, an AI proposal from the last Congress first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: A new security seal of approval is coming to your smart home gadgets
item.description: The White House launched a new cybersecurity safety label, the U.S. Cyber Trust Mark, intended to help consumers make informed decisions on smart device safety.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Copilot AI comes to Microsoft 365 plans: Everything you need to know
item.description: If you’re a Microsoft 365 subscriber with a Personal or Family subscription, Microsoft just flipped a switch and activated Copilot AI features for your account. It’s a new part of your subscription, along with that 1TB of OneDrive storage and access to Office apps like Word and Excel.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to deal with a Big Pile of Mud
item.description: Last week I talked about where those big, unwieldy code bases come from. They don’t appear out of nowhere, and no one ever plans to create one.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: State of JavaScript: Highlights of the JavaScript developer survey
item.description: Getting a complete picture of the multi-headed beast that is the JavaScript ecosystem isn’t easy, but the annual State of JavaScript survey is a good place to start. What makes this survey stand out is that it attracts the input of thousands of working developers and other JavaScript enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 Python web frameworks for beautiful front ends
item.description: Python has long had a presence as a language for server-side frameworks, with support for most every project size or use case. But it’s historically been confined to the back end—there’s no real culture of writing Python for creating front-end, client-side code. At least not yet.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 10 tech upgrades to save your time, privacy and money this year
item.description: Today's tech can help make life so much easier — if you know how to use it. Here are a few tips on the quickest and best ways to make your phone, laptop and other devices work for you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US GPU export limits could bring cold war to AI, data center markets
item.description: The US Biden administration, during its final week in office, issued a rule limiting sales of powerful AI chips to more than 100 countries, to keep the technology out of the hands of Chinese companies and the military there.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
